title: Judiciary
text: Judiciary Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina is the central coordinating body in the field of judiciary in Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ministry is competent for drafting of proposals of laws and sub-law acts and other regulations at the state level, efficient administrative functioning of the judicial bodies of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and cooperation with international criminal courts. Drafting of proposals of laws and sub-law acts Judicial Bodies at the State Level International Courts and Arbitrations Solving of the Unsolved Court Cases Central Data Base Court police Drafting of proposals of laws and sub-law acts Ministry of Justice of BiH is competent for drafting, interpreting and monitoring of the application of the regulations in the field of judiciary at the state level. In order to ensure more efficient judicial system, the Ministry prepares the adequate legal solutions in compliance to the needs of practice and European standards, and provides expert opinions on the drafts of laws and sub-law acts. As it is necessary to remove the noticed legal obstacles in the process of the reform of judiciary, the Ministry constantly monitors and prepares the numerous amendments to the laws, especially the Criminal Procedure Code of BiH (“Official Gazette of BiH”, no. 36/03), Criminal Code of BiH (“Official Gazette of BiH”, no. 37/03)... Consultations in Writing of the Legal Regulations During the preparation of proposals of laws, sub-law acts and other regulations, the Ministry ensures full participation of the public. By conducting different kinds of public consultations, and according to the Rules of Consultations in Writing of the Legal Regulations of the Council of Ministers of BiH the participation of the public in writing of the legal regulations is regulated by the state ministries. Ministry of Justice of BiH is the first state authority that completely follows Public consultations in drafting of laws, sub-law acts and other legal regulations which are important for the public. Judicial Bodies at the State Level Ministry of Justice of BiH ensures the legal conditions for the work of the judiciary institutions at the state level: Court of BiH, Prosecutor’s office of BiH, High Judiciary and Prosecutors Council and Public Attorney’s Office of BiH. Beside the laws for establishing of the judiciary institutions (Law on the Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina, Law on the Prosecutor’s Office of Bosnia and Herzegovina, Law on High Judiciary and Prosecutors Council of Bosnia and Herzegovina аnd Law on Public Attorney’s Office of Bosnia and Herzegovinа) the Ministry is also competent for drafting of process and material laws in criminal and civil area at the state level. International Courts and Arbitrations Ministry of Justice of BiH is authorized for cooperation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and International Criminal Tribunal for Foreign Yugoslavia and International Court of Justice, and other international institutions. The cooperation includes writing and normative defining of the appropriate regulation from this field /Law on the Transfer of Cases from the International Criminal Tribunal for the Foreign Yugoslavia (“Official Gazette of BiH”, no. 61/04)/ and preparation of the periodical reports, information and opinions. The Ministry harmonizes and directs the activities of coordination with international courts, including the monitoring of implementation of rights and obligations that derive from the relevant international obligations and it actively participates at all important international meetings. Solving of the Unsolved Court Cases Ministry of Justice of BiH has a mandate of the leading coordinating authority in solving the problem of large number of unsolved cases. At the moment, the activities of proposing the concrete solutions are in progress and they are supposed to remove the obstacles in establishing the efficient judiciary in Bosnia and Herzegovina. In cooperation with the High Judicial and Prosecutorial Council of BiH, the Action plan for reducing the number of unsolved cases was made, which, among the rest, foresees the establishing of the Coordination Committee and working groups with the task of solving about two million of unsolved cases… Ministry of Justice of BiH actively participates in writing the Strategy for processing of war crimes cases. Central Data Base According to the Criminal Procedure Codes, all courts in Bosnia and Herzegovina are obliged to, without any delay, deliver to the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina all final and legally binding verdicts for criminal offences: Making of false money and bringing it into circulation, Illicit producing, processing and selling of narcotic drugs and poisons, Human Trafficking, Producing and distributing of pornography files, as well as other criminal offences for which is prescribed the centralized database by International Agreements Centralization of data presents extremely useful and important instrument of overview and analyses of the solved cases, and it can be used for different needs of the competent institutions. Court Police Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina is competent for the work of Court Police at the state level. According to the Law on Court police of BiH the court police provides assistance to the Court of BiH and the Prosecutor’s Office of BiH in executing the orders of the Court for the forcedly bringing of the accused persons and witnesses, escorting the convicted persons to the penitentiary institutions, maintaining the order in courtrooms, securing the judges and employees of the Court and Prosecutor’s Office, and internal securing of the Complex of judicial institutions. The court police execute the orders of the Court for extradition, as well as other orders that ensure successful conduction of the court procedures. The Court police at the state level currently has 48 authorized court policemen, who work in accordance to the Law and related Rulebooks, such us /Rulebook on official secret of the officials and employees of the Court police of Bosnia and Herzegovina (“Official Gazette of BiH”, no. 89/07/ and other Rulebooks that regulate the work of the Court Police of Bosnia and Herzegovina.

title: Aim and purpose of the project
anchor_text: Aim and purpose of the project
text: Aim and purpose of the project The project to build the Centre for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions is one of the most significant projects that Bosnia and Herzegovina has undertaken in the last ten years. The aim of the project is to build an institution with the highest level of security, in accordance with the standards of the European Prison Rules and the laws of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The importance of building this institution should be viewed in the context of the overall reform of the BiH judicial system, particularly in the context of adapting the domestic judicial system to best European practices, which is an obligation of BiH under the Stabilisation and Association Agreement. Upon the implementation of the planned project, Bosnia and Herzegovina will gain maximum-security prison capacity of 348 places. The prison will house convicted inmates and detainees of the BiH Court. The State Prison will be the only institution of its kind to meet European prison standards, which is particularly significant given that existing entity prisons are characterised by overcrowding and inadequate capacity. Once the project is implemented, the entity prisons will be able to redirect their priorities to improving the rehabilitation programme for less dangerous inmates, in order to facilitate their reintegration into the community and reduce the number of recidivists. Although establishing an institute at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina requires significant investment, it is estimated that its existence is crucial for the functioning of the entire criminal sanctions enforcement sector, as well as for improving the overall security situation in the country. Commissioning of a new, high-security facility, will ensure the successful implementation of reforms in the areas of the judiciary, the prosecution service and the police, into which domestic structures and the international community have invested significant funds, the success of which would be seriously called into question were such infrastructure not built and only existing correctional facilities used. The construction of a state prison and the establishment of an effective, efficient and sustainable system for the execution of criminal sanctions at the state level are strategic priorities of the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina.

title: State prison construction project
text: State prison construction project Despite numerous obstacles, the construction of the State Prison is in its final phase After all the facilities of the prison complex have been built, the finalization of the works on the external and internal decoration is underway. There are a total of ten facilities, of which facilities A, B, C and D are intended for the accommodation of convicts and detainees, while the other six are service facilities (medical block, sports hall, workshop, kitchen, etc.). As the State Prison will be the most secure prison institution in the region, where the most serious offenders in all forms of crime will serve their sentences, current activities are focused on installing the latest high-security systems, the installation of which, together with the accompanying equipment, is also underway. Given that the works were previously interrupted in the best part of the year for construction work and are currently being carried out in the cold and in bad weather conditions, it is expected that the construction will be completed by the end of May this year. We remind you that previously, in the midst of the construction season, in July 2016, there was an unplanned three-month suspension of works due to a court dispute between the potential contractor for electrical installation works and the main contractor. The state as an investor, namely the Ministry of Justice of BiH, suffered the greatest damage due to the suspension of works, although it was not a party to the proceedings and despite the fact that the dynamic work plan had been fully met until then. So far, donor and loan funds have been used for the construction, and the implementation of the last tranche of the construction loan is underway. We remind you that the construction of the state prison was financed by a loan from the Council of Europe Development Bank in the amount of 19.3 million euros. BiH invested 6.45 million euros, EU donations amounted to 9.15 million euros, technical assistance from the European Commission amounted to 1.7 million euros, a donation from Sweden amounted to two million euros, and the US Government provided 1.1 million dollars for training prison staff. The prison complex will cover an area of 24,000 square meters, and each cell, which will house one prisoner, will be 12 square meters in area. The construction and operation of the future state prison is fully in line with European prison rules and standards. With the implementation of the planned project, Bosnia and Herzegovina will receive maximum security prison facilities, with a capacity of 348 places, of which 298 for prisoners and 50 for detainees. This will significantly relieve the capacity of entity prison facilities and solve the problem of their overcrowding, but also contribute to better resocialization of perpetrators of "lighter" crimes. New tender for the construction of the State Prison The Board of Directors of the Institute for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions, Detention and Other Measures of Bosnia and Herzegovina, at its session held on 31 October 2013, made a decision to cancel the tender for the execution of construction works on the construction of the State Prison. Namely, after a detailed consideration of the Report on the Evaluation of Bids prepared by the Commission for the Selection of Contractors for the Execution of Construction Works, the Board of Directors unanimously concluded that, given that all submitted bids exceed the budget planned for the execution of construction works, the most acceptable option is to make a decision to cancel the tender, in accordance with the provisions of the Council of Europe Development Bank Guidelines. It is expected that a new tender for construction works will be announced in the middle of this month, and that its evaluation will be completed in January 2014. Apart from the postponement of the start of construction, which the Ministry could not influence, there were no other delays in the project itself, as work was carried out on the development of a completely new project, in line with the new European Prison Rules, which was a condition for obtaining a loan from the Council of Europe Development Bank. Work on the new project began in 2010 and since then all activities have been carried out according to the planned deadlines. In the previous period, numerous preparatory works were carried out, such as those required by a capital investment, from extensive technical and construction works, including the construction of an access road and associated lighting, geomechanical testing and demining of the terrain, the construction of test wells, to numerous other activities. The implementation of the contract for the construction of the construction road is also underway, the completion of which is expected by the end of November. In the meantime, approval has been given to the selection of the company that will supervise the execution of the main works. Upon completion of construction, Bosnia and Herzegovina will have the most modern and safest prison facility, compared to all other institutions of similar purpose in the country and in the Balkans. This will be the only prison facility at the BiH level that will meet European standards for accommodating prisoners, which is particularly significant considering the fact that the existing entity prisons are characterized by the problem of overcrowding and inadequate capacity. The evaluation of bids for the construction of the State Prison has been completed The Bid Evaluation Commission for the selection of the contractor for the main works on the construction of the Institution for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions, Detention and Other Measures of BiH has completed the bid review process and submitted a report and recommendation to the Board of Directors. After confirmation by the Board of Directors, the approval of the selection of the contractor should also be given by the Council of Europe Development Bank. At the same time, the Board of Directors approved the report and recommendation of the Bid Evaluation Commission for the selection of the supervision of the execution of the main works on the construction of the Institution for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions, Detention and Other Measures of BiH. The report was submitted to the Development Bank for its opinion in accordance with the provisions of the Framework Financial Agreement between BiH and the Council of Europe Development Bank. If the Development Bank gives its approval, the conditions will be created for the conclusion of the contract for the supervision of the execution of the main works on the construction of the state prison. The implementation of the contract for the construction of the construction road is also underway, the completion of which is expected by the end of November. Submission of bids for the construction of the State Prison by mid-June A large number of domestic and foreign companies have applied to the announced main tender for the construction of the State Prison. The deadline for submitting bids is June 12, and after the closing of the tender procedure, the evaluation of bids and the selection of the contractor will follow. The construction of the facility is expected to begin in the fall of 2013. The creation of conditions for the start of the main works was preceded by numerous activities to prepare the new project and revise it. Extensive technical and construction preparatory works have been completed, including the construction of the access road and associated lighting, geomechanical testing and demining of the terrain, the construction of test wells, and numerous other activities. The new project is fully compliant with European standards and envisages the construction of the most modern and secure prison facility in the region. The facility will have a net area of approximately 24,000 m2 and will house 348 convicts and detainees of the Court of BiH. Placing the most serious convicts in this prison will relieve the capacities of other penal correctional institutions and enable the resocialization of persons convicted of minor crimes. The facility will have minimal energy costs due to maximum energy savings through the use of renewable energy sources and minimal maintenance costs. The original construction deadlines were postponed due to the revision of the old and the development of a new project, i.e. its alignment with the latest prison rules. The costs of developing the new project were fully financed by the European Union. Due to the increase in the total area of the institution, utility costs, security systems, access roads, etc., the value of the construction was also changed to approximately 77.5 million KM, and the project will be financed from donor, loan and domestic funds. By decision of the Council of Ministers of BiH from November last year, the construction of the state prison was declared a long-term capital investment.

title: Sector for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions and the Operation of Penal Institutions
anchor_text: Sector for execution of criminal sanctions and operation of penal institutions
text: Sector for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions and the Operation of Penal Institutions Mahir Delić, Assistant Minister Address: Trg BiH 1, 71000 Sarajevo, Bosna i Hercegovina Phone: + 387 33 28 15 52 Fax: + 387 33 20 16 40 E-mail: mahir.delic@mpr.gov.ba Jurisdictions execution of criminal sanctions, detention and other measures of the Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina, execution of final decisions of foreign courts for acts provided for by the Law of Bosnia and Herzegovina, unless otherwise provided by a special law of Bosnia and Herzegovina or an international treaty to which Bosnia and Herzegovina is a signatory, execution of a criminal sanction of detention or other measure imposed by the Court of BiH against foreign citizens or stateless persons, unless otherwise provided by a special law of BiH or an international treaty to which Bosnia and Herzegovina is a signatory, execution of a criminal sanction imposed in entity courts for acts stipulated by the Criminal Code of BiH, at the request of the convicted person, drafting of laws and bylaws in the field of the system of execution of criminal sanctions at the state level, giving opinions and consent in the implementation of the Law or other regulations, carrying out professional education of prison officials, regulating the conditions of the program and the manner of taking the professional exam for prison officers, inspection supervision over the work of the institution for the execution of criminal sanctions, inspection supervision over the implementation of the process of re-education of convicted persons, inspection supervision over the exercise of the rights of persons deprived of their liberty, proceeding with the request for pardon, transfer and conditional release of convicts.

title: Execution of Criminal Sanctions
text: Execution of Criminal Sanctions Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina is competent for the execution of criminal sanctions, detention and other measures pronounced by the Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The Ministry is competent for the execution of the legally binding decisions of the foreign courts for the acts regulated by the Law of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and execution of the sanctions pronounced by the Court of BiH against the foreign citizens, or persons without citizenship, unless there is a special law of BiH or an international agreement that prescribes some other measures. Detention Unit Institute for Execution of Criminal Sanctions, Detention and other Measures of BiH Supervision over the work of the penitentiary institution /inspection/ Treatment from the Aspect of Human Rights Conditional release Pardon Right to transfer Privileges Professional education Detention Unit The Detention Unit at the state level is intended for the accommodation of the persons serving the detention ordered by the Court of BiH, and persons transferred from the Detention Unit of the ICTY. The majority of cases are the war crimes. The Detention Unit also accommodates the persons against whom the proceeding is conducted by the Department II – Department for the organized crime, economic crime and corruption. At the moment, it is the only institution of this kind in Bosnia and Herzegovina that meets the European standards. It contains 20 cells, 13 square meters each, and there are plans for enlargement for 12 more cells. The Detention Unit of the Court of BiH was opened in May 2005 and at the moment, there are 38 authorized detention officials employed there. They are all obliged to know and apply the European Convention on Human Rights. The Detention police officers are constantly trained in the application of the modern European prison rules. Beside the internal inspections, the rights and obligations of the detainees in this institution are supervised by the European Committee for the Prevention of Torture /CPT/, International Red Cross /IRC/ and other international organizations that deal with the human rights issues. So far, there has been no case of abusing or other kind of restriction of the rights of the detainees. Photo Institute for Execution of Criminal Sanctions, Detention and other Measures of BiH Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina has initiated and is implementing the project of construction of the Institute for execution of the criminal sanctions, detention and other measures of BiH. This will be the only penitentiary institution at the state level of this kind that will meet the European standards for the accommodation of the convicts, which is especially important if we consider the fact that the existing entity penitentiary institutions are overcrowded and with inadequate capacities. The state prison, with the capacity of 300 beds, will accommodate both prisoners and detainees of the Court of BiH. In 2006 and 2007, 1, 6 million euros was approved from the budget of Bosnia and Herzegovina for the works on the infrastructure and for the preparation of the land for the construction. According to the assessment, the construction of the high security prison will cost 14,85 million euros, out of which 2 million euros are approved from the domestic budget funds in 2008. The majority of the financial resources are planned to be provided from the donor and credit funds. More Supervision over the work of the penitentiary Inspection service of the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina supervises the work of the Detention Unit at the state level as well as all other penitentiary institutions in Bosnia and Herzegovina in order to identify the conditions and circumstances in which the detainees or prisoners serve the detention or prison sentence according to the decision or verdict of the Court of BiH. Inspector is a civil servant with special authorities and the measures ordered by the inspector are obligatory for the penitentiary managers. The inspection includes the control of the working plan and program of the penitentiaries, correct application of the Law on Execution of Criminal Sanctions, Detention and other Measures and other regulations, that regulate the rightful and correct treatment of the detainees and prisoners, and realizing their guaranteed rights. In order to regulate the internal life in penitentiary institutions and realizing of the rights and duties, the Ministry of Justice of BiH has made 32 rulebooks, instructions, that is decisions which contain over 1000 legal dispositions that regulate the complete work of the penitentiary institutions, from the moment of admission of the detainees or prisoners, to the moment of their release from detainment or serving the prison sentence. Treatment from the Aspect of Human Rights Treatment with the detainees and prisoners from the aspect of human rights includes: Human treatment and respect of the human dignity; Maintaining the mental and physical health; Preventing of the torture and inhumane or degrading treatment or punishment; Preventing the discrimination based on the ethnic origin, race, color of skin, sex, sexual orientation, language, religion or belief, political or other opinion, national and social origin, economic or other status; Right to religion; Right to information; Right to health protection; Right to clothing; Right to writing and receiving letters; Right to submitting requests, lodging complaints, and other documents; Right to enjoy an open space; Right to use the library; Right to free time activities (sport, cultural etc.); Right to work according to the possibilities of a penitentiary institution; Right to protection and insurance at work; Right to salary; Right to education and organizing of the classes for general and professional education and qualifying; Right to annual vacation; Right to communicate, visits, as well as other rights regulated by the Law and sub-law regulations. Human rights of the detainees or prisoners are guaranteed by the Criminal Code of BiH and Law on Execution of Criminal Sanctions, Detention and other Measures, which is based on the valid international standards. Some of the documents that regulate and establish the principles and basis in the prison system are: European Prison Rules, United Nations Standard Minimum Rules for the Treatment of Prisoners, European Convention for the Prevention of Torture and Inhumane or Degrading Treatment or Punishment, European Convention on Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms... Conditional release Conditional release of the convicts sentenced by the Court of BiH is approved by the Conditional Release Commission at the state level, which is appointed by the Minister of Justice of BiH. The commission is consisted of 5 members, 1 representative of the Court of BiH, 1 representative of the Ministry of Justice of BiH and 3 independent members from the relevant professional field. The Commission is appointed for the period of five years and it is independent in its work. Pardon Article 119 of the Criminal Code of BiH states that pardon for the criminal offences prescribed by this Code can be granted by the decision of the Presidency of BiH pursuant to the Law. Therefore, the Law of Bosnia and Herzegovina on Pardon, was adopted /”Official Gazette of BiH”, No. 93/05/, which was determined by the competent authority for granting pardons, kinds of pardons, initiating of the proceedings and other matters. The article 21 prescribes that in order to act upon the request efficiently, the Minister of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina appoints the Commission for Pardons which acts from the moment of submitting of the request, establishes the competence, collects the relevant data and information, and makes the report about the request which, together with the request and reasoned proposal of the Ministry of Justice, delivers to the Presidency of BiH which decides on the request. Right to Transfer If an inmate has served ½ of the sentence and spends his/her vacation within the family, he may, according to the law, submit a request for transfer to the other penitentiary institution in the entity where he/she lives or has got a residence. The right to transfer is regulated by the Rulebook on criteria for sending convicts to serve the prison sentences (“Official Gazette of BiH” no. 34/05) and Rulebook on changes and supplements to the Rulebook on criteria for sending convicts to serve the prison sentences (“Official Gazette of BiH” no. 83/07). The decision on the transfer is made by the Minister of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. Privileges The Law on Execution of Criminal Sanctions contains the regulations which enable granting privileges to the convicted persons for good behavior and devotion to work. The privileges can be used within and outside of the penitentiary institution, and are approved by the manager of penitentiary institution. Rulebook on privileges and vacation of prisoners that serve the prison sentence in the Institute for execution of criminal sanctions, detention and other measures of Bosnia and Herzegovina (“Official Gazette of BiH” no. 34/05), Rulebook on changes and supplements to the Rulebook on privileges and vacation of prisoners that serve the prison sentence in the Institute for execution of criminal sanctions, detention and other measures of Bosnia and Herzegovina (“Official Gazette of BiH” no. 38/06) and Rulebook on changes and supplements to the Rulebook on privileges and vacation of prisoners that serve the prison sentence in the Institute for execution of criminal sanctions, detention and other measures of Bosnia and Herzegovina (“Official Gazette of BiH” no. 11/08) regulate the procedure and method of granting privileges, their duration, kind, as well as the way of their possible limitation. Professional education The Law on Execution of Criminal Sanctions, in a special article, emphasizes the professional education and prescribes that the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina, in cooperation with entity Ministries of Justice, will set certain standards for each category of the prison staff and regarding that it may organize courses, seminars, consulting, and other kinds of professional development, in order to enable the prison staff to satisfy given standards. Therefore, the Rulebook and program of the professional exams (“Official Gazette of BiH” no. 56/05) were made and related changes, according to which is organized the procedure of professional exams for the prison staff of the Detention Unit at the state level. Through the different projects, the prison police officers are constantly trained on the implementation of the modern European prison rules.

title: Main features of the project
text: Main features of the project The main features of the project make the BiH Institution for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions, Detention and Other Measures more modern and secure than any other institution of a similar purpose in the country or on the Balkans. This will be the only prison facility at the level of BiH that will meet European standards for the accommodation of convicted prisoners, which is particularly significant considering that the existing entity prisons are characterised by overcrowding and inadequate capacity. The BiH Centre for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions for Detainees and Other Measures, with a capacity of 348 places, will house convicted prisoners (298 people) and detainees (50 persons) of the BiH Courts. These are the most dangerous convicts and detainees, war criminals and other serious offenders in all forms of crime. The facility will have a net floor area of approximately 24,000 m². A special block will be built for aggressive inmates, and if necessary, all necessary medical care will be provided without them having to leave the prison grounds. The initial project estimated that the construction costs would amount to around 30 million KM. However, following an analysis, this project was rejected as it did not meet European standards, which was one of the conditions for obtaining the loan. The construction costs, according to the new project, which is aligned with European prison standards, were estimated at around 56 million KM. By a decision of the Council of Ministers of BiH in November 2010, the Project for the Construction of the Institution for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions, Detention and Other Measures of BiH was included in the Programme of Multi-Year Capital Investments. Compliance with the latest standards, in terms of providing around 60 m² of gross floor area per prisoner (the cell area is 12 m²), has increased the total area of the facility, and consequently the estimated cost of constructing the new facility has also increased compared to the initial plan. This amount also includes, which was not the case in the original project, the construction of access roads, the installation of high-level security systems, the delivery of equipment, the normalisation of prison life, as well as the training of future employees. The new project also includes energy efficiency and environmental protection measures, which were not foreseen in the previous cost estimate. The project to build the Institution for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions for Detention and Other Measures in Bosnia and Herzegovina is financed from domestic, donor and loan funds. The structure of the Project's sources of financing is as follows: 6.45 MEUR from the budget of the institutions of BiH (land value 1.3 MEUR) 11 million EUR in donor funds (IPA and the Kingdom of Sweden) 1.7 million euros in technical assistance from the European Commission 19.3 million euros loan from the Council of Europe Development Bank 1.1 million USD for training future employees (US Embassy in BiH)

title: Activities so far
text: Activities so far Work on the new project for the construction of the Prison Service began in 2010. The new project was drawn up at the request of the European Commission, which was a condition for obtaining a loan from the Council of Europe Development Bank. The previous project was rejected because it did not meet European standards. Since the start of the new project's implementation, all activities have been carried out according to the planned schedule. During the previous period, numerous preparatory works required for a capital investment were carried out, ranging from extensive technical to construction works, namely: Preparation of occupational health and safety and fire protection documentation. Preparation of a report on geomechanical soil tests and geothermal well tests, Construction of an access road with associated infrastructure and installation of street lighting, completion of technical documentation and the technical documentation review process, Demining of part of the site designated for the construction of the State Prison, Obtaining a building permit. The primary activity of the Project Implementation Unit is currently related to conducting the tender for the execution of works and supervising the works. The Unit prepared the tender documentation within the stipulated deadlines, and the successful completion of these activities was a necessary prerequisite for the further implementation of the project. The main objective of the Unit with regard to these activities is to ensure a level playing field in the public procurement process, for both domestic and international bidders, whilst taking into account the scale and complexity of this project. You can follow the latest information on the project's progress and status, as well as current activities, in the 'Latest News' section.

title: Project Implementation Unit
text: Project Implementation Unit The Project Implementation Unit for the construction of the BiH Centre for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions, Detention and Other Measures (JIP) was established by a decision of the Council of Ministers of BiH and began operating on 1 February 2011. The unit is the operational body responsible for implementing the construction of the Centre for the Execution of Criminal Sanctions, Detention and Other Measures of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The establishment and functioning of the Project Implementation Unit is one of the obligations that BiH undertook by signing the Framework Financial Agreement between the Council of Europe Development Bank and BiH. The Ministry of Justice of BiH, in cooperation with donors and the creditor who closely monitor this project and the fulfilment of transparency and efficiency standards, supervises the work of the Unit, both directly and through the Management Board and the Supervisory Board. The Governing Board and the Supervisory Board are composed of representatives of the state Ministry of Justice and of finance and treasury, as well as of the entity ministries of justice appointed by the Council of Ministers of BiH. The Supervisory Board primarily monitors the use of financial resources. Contact Name and surname: Zoran Bašić, director JIP-a Phone: + 387 57 490-135 E-mail: zoran.basic@mpr.gov.ba

title: International legal assistance and cooperation
text: International legal assistance and cooperation The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina is the central authority for communication with other countries in providing international legal assistance in criminal and civil matters. International legal assistance encompasses all communication between the judicial authorities of Bosnia and Herzegovina and those abroad. International Legal Assistance in Criminal Matters International legal assistance in civil matters International treaties and conventions Professional training International Legal Assistance in Criminal Matters International assistance in criminal matters includes: all actions taken by the competent authority of a foreign state at the request of the domestic competent authority and vice versa procedures for extradition, the transfer of convicted persons, and the transfer of criminal proceedings from one state to another other procedures governed by special international conventions and treaties International legal assistance in civil matters For the purposes of providing international legal assistance, civil matters include both family and other legal matters and relationships. In accordance with the Convention on the Enforcement of Maintenance Obligations Abroad and the Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction, the Ministry handles all communication between judicial authorities in Bosnia and Herzegovina and abroad. International treaties and conventions International legal assistance and cooperation are based on multilateral and bilateral international treaties that are binding on Bosnia and Herzegovina. The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina conducts the full procedures for concluding international agreements in the field of international legal assistance, as well as the procedures for acceding to international conventions. This applies in particular to: Matters concerning the extradition of accused and convicted persons, action on requests for the enforcement of foreign court decisions in criminal, civil and other matters, and Actions on requests for international criminal, civil and other legal assistance. By acceding to the European Conventions in the field of criminal law (extradition, transfer of sentenced persons, transfer of criminal proceedings and international legal assistance in criminal matters), the procedure for providing international legal assistance has been significantly improved and simplified. By the Succession Agreement, Bosnia and Herzegovina assumed all international treaties in this field that were binding on the former SFR of Yugoslavia. By selecting the link to International Conventions and Treaties, you can gain a complete overview of the review and content of bilateral international treaties and other international instruments in the field of international legal assistance that Bosnia and Herzegovina has to date signed or concluded by exchange of notes with certain states of the world, and has assumed on the basis of the Agreement on Succession. Professional training As the need for International Legal Assistance increases enormously year on year, the Ministry is making every effort and is professionally engaged in improving the efficiency of the entire system in this area. The Sector for International Assistance and Cooperation conducts practical training for judges and prosecutors throughout BiH. With the expert publication International Legal Assistance I and II, the training of judges and prosecutors has received the highest ratings for its contribution to improving the overall system of international legal assistance.

title: Decision-making
text: Decision-making Under the provisions of the Convention, the procedure for these requests is urgent. However, given the complexity of the issues addressed by the Convention, the possibility of seeking legal remedies, and the different legal systems and various forms of internal organisation of the States Parties to the Convention, the procedure itself may take a relatively long time. The Convention also provides for the possibility that the competent court may make a decision refusing the request for the return of an unlawfully removed or retained child. The decision on the return or contact with the child is taken by the court with both subject-matter and territorial jurisdiction in the State where the child is located. Against the first-instance decision on the (non-)return or the granting of contact with the child, all legal remedies provided for by the domestic legislation of the contracting State may be pursued.

title: Request to return or see the child
text: Request to return or see the child The request from Bosnia and Herzegovina for the return or for contact with the child, together with the accompanying documentation, is submitted to the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina with an attached translation into the language of the requested State. The request and the necessary documentation must be translated by an authorised court interpreter. With a request for the return or for contact with a minor child, the following documentation must be provided: a completed set of forms, information on the identity of the applicant, information about the child's identity, information on the identity of the person alleged to have taken or detained the child, all relevant facts on which the request for the return or contact with the child is based, other facts that can provide more information regarding the child's current place of residence and the identity of the person with whom the child is living, a court decision or agreements made before a judicial or administrative authority, concerning the award of custody of the child to one of the spouses /if such decisions exist/. extract from the register of births for the minor child and/or certificate of citizenship of the minor child, Photographs of the minor child and of the person with whom the child is currently staying. Address: Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina Sector for International and Inter-Entity Legal Assistance and Cooperation Square of Bosnia and Herzegovina No. 1

title: Proceedings in case of child abduction
anchor_text: Action in the event of child abduction
text: Proceedings in case of child abduction In cases where a person from Bosnia and Herzegovina seeks the return or contact of a child who is in one of the States party to the Convention, The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina, together with the request for contact with the child or the request for the return of the child, also forwards the necessary documentation to the foreign central authority in accordance with the Hague Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction. The Ministry notifies the applicant of the measures taken by the foreign central executive body, as well as of the relevant information obtained during the procedure. A similar situation arises when a request for the return of a child is made by a person from outside Bosnia and Herzegovina, while the child is in Bosnia and Herzegovina. In that case, once a request is received from the competent foreign central authority, The Ministry of Justice of BiH determines whether the request has been made in accordance with the Convention and, if it is in order, forwards it to the competent entity's Ministry of Justice, which in turn refers it to the court with actual and territorial jurisdiction for resolution. If the request is incomplete, the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina will not dismiss it but will, through the central executing authority of the requesting State, request that the request be supplemented and completed in accordance with the provisions of the Convention. You can find a list of all central authorities of the Member States here.

title: International child abductions
text: International child abductions The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina is the central executive authority for communication between judicial bodies in Bosnia and Herzegovina and abroad in cases of international child abduction. International child abduction refers to the unlawful removal and/or retention of a child in another country. Bosnia and Herzegovina is a signatory to the Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction, The Hague, 25 October 1980, which was adopted by the notification on succession to the international treaties of the SFRY. The Convention regulates the procedure in cases of the unlawful removal or retention of children abroad by a parent or other blood relative. Furthermore, the Convention also applies to the realisation of the right of a parent or other blood relatives to have contact with the child.

text: Countries where the convention applies Except for Bosnia and Herzegovina, the Convention on the Enforcement of Maintenance Obligations Abroad of 20 June 1956. the Convention of 20 June 1956 on the Enforcement of Maintenance Obligations Abroad also applies in relation to Algeria, Argentina, Australia, Austria, Barbados, Belarus, Belgium, Bolivia, Brazil, Burkina Faso, Cambodia, Cape Verde, the Central African Republic, Chile, China, Colombia, Croatia, Cuba, Cyprus, the Czech Republic, Germany, Greece, Guatemala, Haiti, the Holy See, Hungary, Ireland, Israel, Italy, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Liberia, Luxembourg, Mexico, Morocco, the Netherlands, New Zealand, Nigeria, Norway, Pakistan, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Serbia, Montenegro, Seychelles, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sri Lanka, Suriname, Sweden, Switzerland, Macedonia, Tunisia, Turkey, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, Uruguay. In accordance with the provisions of the Convention, each State Party shall, by means of its domestic legislation, prescribe which evidence is required under its national law to establish a claim for maintenance. Each State Party shall notify the Secretary-General of the United Nations of the said evidence. The names of the central authorities in the States party to the Hague Convention of 23 November 2007 on the International Enforcement of Child Maintenance and Other Family Maintenance Obligations are available at the following link: https://www.hcch.net/en/instruments/conventions/authorities1/?cid=131

title: Procedure for exercising rights
text: Procedure for exercising rights Communication between the member states of the Convention on the Enforcement of Maintenance Claims Abroad is carried out through central bodies. Maintenance creditors from Bosnia and Herzegovina address their maintenance claims to the competent entity bodies dealing with social welfare, namely: the Federal Ministry of Labor and Social Policy, the Ministry of Health and Social Protection of the RS and the Judicial Commission of the Brčko District of BiH. After that, the competent bodies submit the claims, with the necessary documentation, to the Ministry of Justice of BiH. The obligation of the Ministry of Justice is to determine whether the submitted claims have been made in accordance with the provisions of the Convention, and whether they are complete. If the claims are not made in accordance with the provisions of the Convention, the Ministry of Justice will return them to the competent entity administrative bodies for correction and/or amendment. The Ministry of Justice will forward duly submitted maintenance claims submitted through the competent entity administrative bodies to the competent central body in the debtor's state, and continuously monitor their implementation. If the alimony creditor mistakenly submits a request directly to the Ministry of Justice, instead of to the competent body, the Ministry will send such a request to the competent entity body, and that body is obliged to act on it as if the request was addressed to it. The Ministry, through the competent entity body, will inform the alimony creditor about all information related to a specific alimony request.

title: Alimony
text: Alimony The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina is the central state authority for the implementation of the Convention on the Enforcement of Maintenance Claims Abroad of 1956. The Convention facilitates the initiation of maintenance proceedings and the enforcement of decisions abroad. The enforcement of maintenance claims abroad implies the exercise of the rights of a person whose legal guardian is located in another country. The Convention on the Enforcement of Maintenance Claims Abroad applies in cases where the maintenance creditor is located in the territory of one Contracting Party (the creditor's country) and the maintenance debtor is located in the territory and under the jurisdiction of the other Contracting Party (the debtor's country). The 2007 Hague Convention on the International Enforcement of Child Support and Other Forms of Family Maintenance has been binding on Bosnia and Herzegovina since 1 February 2013. It entered into force on 1 January 2013. It is important to note that the USA signed the 2007 Hague Convention on 23 September 2007 and that it only entered into force at the beginning of 2017. The aforementioned Convention, which is binding on Bosnia and Herzegovina and all its authorities, has a humanitarian character and aims to facilitate the realization of alimony, i.e. the realization of the right of alimony creditors to legal maintenance, therefore it is necessary for Bosnia and Herzegovina and all its authorities to ensure its smooth implementation. The reason for adopting an additional legal instrument was to correct the shortcomings of certain solutions contained in the already existing Convention, but also to resolve problems that arise from its application in practice. The adoption of the new Convention has expanded and regulated in more detail the entire area of realization of the right to maintenance in international cases. It contains most of the most efficient solutions and actually represents a better way of resolving the above cases. Thus, the preamble to the Hague Convention already emphasizes its purpose: to promote cooperation between States in the international enforcement of child support and other forms of family maintenance and to provide procedures that will yield results and that are accessible, expeditious, efficient, cost-effective, appropriate and fair. In this sense, the subject matter of the Hague Convention is also more broadly defined (a comprehensive system of cooperation, ensuring that maintenance decisions are recognized and enforced, while requiring effective measures for the prompt enforcement of decisions) as well as its application (parent-child maintenance obligations towards a person under the age of 21 and spousal maintenance). After the maintenance creditor submits a request for maintenance to the central authority of the state of the maintenance creditor, the request is forwarded to the central authority of the state of the debtor, unless it is determined to be unfounded. The central authority of the creditor's state may submit to the central authority of the debtor's state its opinion on the justification of the claim for maintenance and may recommend that the creditor be provided with free legal assistance and exemption from costs.

title: Management
text: Management The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina ensures a reliable, cost-effective, transparent and professional public administration that represents the interests of citizens and strives to apply EU best practice. In addition to legislative activities, the Ministry is also responsible, among other things, for the registration of citizens' associations and for maintaining the registers of citizens' associations and non-governmental organisations operating in the territory of BiH. Registration of associations and foundations Registration of churches and religious communities Registration of legal entities established by institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina Approval for the use of the name of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the name of a legal entity Approval for the production of seals of institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina Submission and recognition of professional administrative exams Providing opinions on acts Registration of associations and foundations The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina is responsible for registering associations, foundations, offices or representative offices of foreign and international associations and foundations in the Register, as well as for amendments to the Register. Registration in the register of associations, foundations, and foreign and international associations and foundations is carried out in accordance with the Law on Associations and Foundations of Bosnia and Herzegovina ('Official Gazette of BiH', no. 32/01), the Law on Amendments to the Law on Associations and Foundations of Bosnia and Herzegovina ("Official Gazette of BiH" no. 42/03), the Law on Amendments and Supplements to the Law on Associations and Foundations of Bosnia and Herzegovina (" Official Gazette of BiH, no. 63/08) and the Law on Amendments and Supplementations to the Law on Associations and Foundations of Bosnia and Herzegovina ("Official Gazette of BiH", no. 76/11), and in accordance with Regulations on the Method of Keeping the Register of Associations and Foundations of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Foreign and International Associations and Foundations and Other Non-profit Organisations (''Official Gazette of BiH'', no: 68/18). Registration of churches and religious communities The registration of churches and religious communities is carried out in accordance with the Law on Freedom of Religion and the Legal Status of Churches and Religious Communities in Bosnia and Herzegovina ('Official Gazette of BiH', no. 5/04) and the Regulation on the Establishment and Maintenance of the Unified Register for the Entry of Churches and Religious Communities, their Unions and Organisational Forms in Bosnia and Herzegovina ("Official Gazette of BiH", no. 46/04). Churches and religious communities are communities, institutions and organisations of believers, established in accordance with their own regulations, teachings, beliefs, traditions and practices, which have legal capacity and are entered in the Register of Churches and Religious Communities in Bosnia and Herzegovina. The register of existing and new churches and religious communities, their associations and internal organisational forms, is maintained by the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. Registration of legal entities established by institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina Legal entities that establish institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina are registered with the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina in accordance with the Law on the Registration of Legal Entities that Establish Institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina ('Official Gazette of BiH', no. 33/02 and 37/03). Approval for the use of the name of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the name of a legal entity The Law on the Use and Protection of the Name of Bosnia and Herzegovina (Official Gazette of BiH no. 30/03, 42/04, 50/08 and 76/11) regulates the conditions and manner of use and protection of the name of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The authorisation to use the name of Bosnia and Herzegovina is granted by the Council of Ministers of Bosnia and Herzegovina, on the proposal of the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina, in accordance with Article 6 of this Law. An application to use the name of Bosnia and Herzegovina may be submitted by institutions, citizens' associations and other legal entities that wish to include the name of Bosnia and Herzegovina in their registered name. The application for the issuance of approval to use the name of Bosnia and Herzegovina, in accordance with Article 5 of the aforementioned Law, must include: a) name of the applicant; b) the title proposed; c) the founding act and the statutes; d) the applicant's annual activity plan and annual financial plan and e) explanation of the reason for submitting the request. The justification referred to in paragraph 1(e) of this article shall contain a description of the activities to be carried out and the manner in which the applicant's activities and operations will be conducted throughout the territory of Bosnia and Herzegovina, with a detailed statement of the reasons justifying the request for approval to use the name of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the applicant's name. In accordance with Article 6(2) of the Law on the Use and Protection of the Name of Bosnia and Herzegovina, the procedure for issuing authorisation for the use of the name of Bosnia and Herzegovina is conducted in accordance with the provisions of this Law and the Law on Administrative Procedure. Administrative fee Approval for the creation of a seal The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina approves the making of seals for the institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina in accordance with the Law on the Seal of Institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina ("Official Gazette of BiH", no. 12/98 and 14/03) and the Instruction on the procedure and method of making, the method of keeping and the content of records of seals, the destruction and decommissioning of seals of institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina ("Official Gazette of BiH", no. 6/12). This Act regulates the form and appearance of the seal of the institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina, as well as its use by those institutions. The seal of the institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina confirms the authenticity of official documents. Submission and recognition of professional administrative examinations The taking of professional examinations is regulated by the Decision on the Conditions and Procedure for Taking the Professional Administrative Examination for Trainees, Persons with Higher Education and Employees at the Level of Bosnia and Herzegovina ("Official Gazette of BiH", no. 104/10) and the Decision on Amendments and Supplements to the Decision on the Conditions and Method of Taking the Professional Administrative Examination for Trainees, Persons with Higher Education and Employees at the Level of BiH ("Official Gazette of BiH", no. 105/12). Recognition of previously passed professional administrative& Recognition of previously passed professional administrative exams, or exemption from the obligation to sit the professional administrative exam at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina, is carried out on the basis of a comparison between the syllabus under which the person passed the professional exam and the Programme of the professional administrative exam for trainees, civil servants and employees at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina. Providing opinions on acts The Sector for State-Level Administration provides opinions on draft and proposed laws, decisions, instructions, conclusions, decisions and other regulatory acts concerning matters relating to sanctions, the organisation and functioning of the administrative bodies of Bosnia and Herzegovina and their mutual relationships, as well as all regulations on the internal organisation of institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina. Administrative fees for giving opinions

title: Associations
text: Associations The Law on Associations and Foundations of Bosnia and Herzegovina (''Official Gazette of BiH'', no: 32/01, 42/03, 63/08, 76/11 and 94/16), (hereinafter: the Act), regulates the establishment, bodies and legal status of associations and foundations, registration, assets, dissolution and deletion from the Register, status changes and other matters significant for the work of associations and foundations that choose to register in accordance with this Act at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina. An unofficial consolidated version of the Act is available in the documents. Registration in the register of associations, foundations, foreign and international associations and foundations is carried out in accordance with the Law and the Regulation on the manner of keeping the register of associations and foundations of Bosnia and Herzegovina and foreign international associations and foundations and other non-profit organisations (''Official Gazette of BiH'', no: 68/18), (hereinafter: the Regulation).

title: How to establish an association?
text: How to establish an association? An association may be established by at least three founders. The founders of the association may be natural persons who are citizens of Bosnia and Herzegovina and foreigners who have their residence in Bosnia and Herzegovina, or legal persons registered in Bosnia and Herzegovina. An association may be established by physical and legal persons, but the number of founders must not be fewer than three. For the establishment of an association that meets the conditions for registration, it is mandatory to adopt a founding act. Actions taken between the establishment and the registration of an association can create obligations only for those who took those actions. The association is considered to be established for an indefinite period, unless otherwise provided by a special law, the founding act and the statutes. In order for the association to be entered in the Ministry's Register, the Founding Assembly of the association is required to adopt: the founding act, or the decision to establish the association; statutes of the association the decision on the appointment of the association's governing body. The decision to establish the association – the founding act – contains: a) the names and surnames, or the names, of the founders and their places of residence, or registered offices; b) the name of the association; c) the registered office and address of the association; d) the aims and activities of the association; e) the name and address of the person authorised to carry out the registration in the Register; f) the name, surname and signatures of the founders; g) the number and date of the founding act. When the association's founder is a legal entity, the founding act of the association is signed by its representative, who appends their signature and the legal entity's seal to the full legal name. The decision to establish the association, i.e. the founding act, is signed by the founder and the President of the Founding Assembly of the association, and bears the date and protocol number, e.g. 01/18. The association's statutes contain: a) the name of the association, its abbreviated name if any, and the association's address; b) the aims and activities of the association; c) procedure for admitting and expelling members; d) the association's bodies, the manner in which they are elected, the powers they have, quorum and voting rules, the duration of the term of office, the person authorised to convene the general assembly, the conditions and manner of dissolution, or cessation of activities; e) rules for acquiring, using and disposing of the association's funds, as well as the body authorised to supervise the use of those funds; e1) the manner of submitting the financial report and the report on work; f) the public likes it; g) the procedure for amending and supplementing the statutes, the authorisation and manner of adopting other general acts; h) a description of the shape and content of the association's seal and emblem; i) representing the association; j) the conditions and procedure for the merger, division or dissolution of the association, including any special quorum or rules for achieving a qualified majority in the voting process; and k) the procedure for the disposal of the remaining assets or other resources in the event of the association's dissolution or cessation of activities. The statute is signed by the President of the Founding Assembly of the association and bears the date and protocol number, e.g. 02/18. PUBLICITY OF THE REGISTER An association or foundation acquires legal personality on the day it is entered in the Register by the Ministry. The Register is open to the public and any interested person may, in person or by post, request a copy of any entry in the Register or any document from the file, in accordance with the provisions of the Law on Freedom of Access to Information in Bosnia and Herzegovina. All decisions issued by the Ministry concerning the registration in the Register, the registration of amendments to the Register, and the registration of deletion from the Register are published in the 'Official Gazette of BiH' at the expense of the registered entities and on the Ministry's website.

title: How to establish a foundation?
text: How to establish a foundation? A foundation is a legal entity with no membership, whose purpose is to manage specific assets in the public interest or for charitable purposes. The founders of the foundation do not have to be citizens of Bosnia and Herzegovina, nor legal entities registered in Bosnia and Herzegovina. A foundation may be established by a unilateral declaration of intent, by contract, by will, by legacy or by any other appropriate legal act. The foundation's founding act contains: The foundation's founding act contains: a) the name and surname, or the name and legal form of the founder and their place of residence, or registered office; b) the name of the foundation; c) the registered office and address of the foundation; d) the aims and activities of the foundation; e) the amount of cash or other assets to be contributed by the founder, which shall not be less than two thousand convertible marks; f) the name and address of the person authorised to carry out the tasks of registration in the Register; g) the first name, surname and signature of the founder; h) the number and date of the founding act. The decision to establish the foundation is signed by the founder and bears the date and protocol number; if the foundation's founder is a legal entity, the decision is signed by the person authorised to represent that entity. The foundation's statutes contain: a) the name, any abbreviated name if applicable, and the registered office of the foundation; b) the aims and activities of the foundation; c) the foundation's bodies, the manner in which they are elected, the powers they have, quorum and voting rules, the duration of their term of office, the person authorised to convene the board of directors, the conditions and manner of dissolution, or cessation of activities; d) rules for the management and use of the foundation's assets; potential beneficiaries of the foundation's funds; e) procedures for the amendment and supplementation of the statutes, as well as the authorisation and procedures for the adoption of other general acts; f) the conditions and procedure for the merger and division or dissolution of the foundation, including any voting rules requiring a special quorum or a qualified majority; g) criteria for the disposal of the foundation's assets in the event of its dissolution; and h) description of the shape and content of the foundation's seal and emblem The foundation's statute is adopted by the Board of Directors of the foundation and is signed by the Chair of the Board of Directors of the foundation. The statute contains the date and protocol number.

title: Foundation registration
text: Foundation registration The application for registration of an association or foundation is submitted to the Ministry on Form 1, together with a list of the association's or foundation's founders on Form 1.1. The application is completed electronically, in Latin script or Cyrillic script, in one of the three official languages in use in Bosnia and Herzegovina, in the appropriate fields. The application is signed by the person authorised in the founding act of the association or foundation to submit the application for registration in the register on behalf of the founder. The application and its attachments must be submitted in the original or in a certified copy no older than three months, in a single copy, except for the statute, which must be submitted in two copies. In the application for the registration of an association or foundation, the applicant classifies the association or foundation in accordance with its objectives and activities. The association or foundation independently determines its objectives and activities in accordance with the Constitution and the Law. An association or foundation independently classifies itself, according to its own choice, into one or more areas of activity, and the information is entered into the register of associations and foundations. Along with the application for registration of the foundation in the register (Form 1), containing the information referred to in Article 7(1) of these regulations and the list of the foundation's founders (Form 1.1), the applicant shall attach: a) the foundation's founding act, in accordance with Article 23 of the Act; b) certified copies of the identity cards of the founders who are citizens of Bosnia and Herzegovina, or passport – of the foreign founder, or an extract from the register for a legal person founder or another legal act proving the status of the legal person founder of the foundation; c) the foundation's statutes in two copies, in accordance with Article 24 of the Act; d) a personal identity document, or an extract from the register, proving the identity of the founder, where the foundation is established by a unilateral declaration of intent, contract, will, legacy or other appropriate legal act; e) a certified statement from the applicant, made under full moral, material and criminal liability, declaring that the foundation in formation has not been registered under the same name with another registration authority in Bosnia and Herzegovina; f) the decision of the founder on the appointment of the decision of the founder on the appointment of the foundation's board of directors, which contains the names, surnames and addresses of the appointed persons, their duties on the board, the duration of their term of office, the date on which the decision comes into force, and the date, number and signature of the foundation's founder; g) statements from the members of the foundation's board of directors confirming their acceptance of membership, certified by the competent authority; h) the decision of the foundation's board of directors on the appointment of the person authorised to represent the foundation, which contains the first name, surname and address of the person appointed to represent the foundation, the duration of the term of office, the date on which the decision comes into force, the date, number and signature of the chairman of the foundation's board of directors; i) a certified signature of the person representing the foundation; j) a specimen of the foundation's sign, if prescribed by a provision of the articles of association; k) translation of the foundation's name into the languages in official use in Bosnia and Herzegovina, pursuant to Article 7(5) and (7) of the Act, certified by an authorised court interpreter; l) translation of the foundation's name into a foreign language, if the foundation, in accordance with its statutes, also has a name in a foreign language, certified by an authorised court interpreter; m) consent under Article 7(6) of the Act if the name of a foundation includes the name of a natural person; n) consent of the competent authority of the city, municipality or settlement for the use of the name of the city, municipality or settlement in the foundation's name; o) the amount of cash or other assets which the founder contributes upon the establishment of the foundation; p) proof of payment of the administrative fee, and r) other evidence in accordance with the foundation's statutes.

title: Administrative fee
text: Administrative fee The applicant must submit with the application proof of payment of the administrative fee for the records and procedures in the relevant administrative matter with the Ministry at the time of submission. title: Required documentation
text: Required documentation For the registration of an association, as well as an association of a higher level (federation), it is necessary to submit Form 1 with a list of founders, Form 1.1, and the Guide for the Classification of Associations and Foundations to the Ministry. Along with the application for registration in the register of associations (Form 1), containing the information from Article 7(1) of the regulations and the list of the association's founders (Form 1.1), the applicant shall attach: a) minutes of the association's founding assembly; b) the founding act of the association, in accordance with Article 11 of the Act; c) certified copies of the identity cards of founders who are citizens of Bosnia and Herzegovina, that is, the passport and proof of authorised residence in Bosnia and Herzegovina – for a foreign founder, or an extract from the register for a legal person founder or another legal act proving the legal person status of the founder of the association; d) the association's statutes in two copies, in accordance with Article 12 of the Act; e) a certified statement from the applicant, made under full moral, material and criminal liability, declaring that the association in formation has not been registered under the same name with another registration authority in Bosnia and Herzegovina; f) the decision on the appointment of the association's bodies, containing the first names, surnames and addresses of the appointed persons, their duties within the association, the duration of their term of office, the date of entry into force of the decision, and the date, number and signature of the President of the founding assembly of the association; g) a certified signature of the person representing the association; i) a copy of the association's sign, if prescribed by a provision of the articles of association; j) translation of the association's name into the languages in official use in Bosnia and Herzegovina, in accordance with Article 7, paragraphs (5) and (7) of the Act, certified by an authorised court interpreter; k) a translation of the association's name into the foreign language, if the association has a name in that language in accordance with its articles of association, certified by an authorised court interpreter; l) consent under Article 7(6) of the Act if the name of an association includes the name of a natural person; m) consent of the competent authority of the city, municipality or settlement for the use of the name of the city, municipality or settlement in the association's name; n) proof of payment of the administrative fee o) other evidence in accordance with the association's statutes.
