title: Required documentation
text: Required documentation A foreign or international association or foundation and other non-profit organisations (hereinafter: foreign non-governmental organisation) may establish an office, a representative office or another organisational form (hereinafter: office or representative office) in Bosnia and Herzegovina. The application for the registration of an office or representation of a foreign non-governmental organisation is submitted to the Ministry on Form 4. The application with attachments is completed on a computer, in Latin script or Cyrillic script, in one of the official languages of Bosnia and Herzegovina, within the relevant section. The request and its attachments must be submitted in a single copy. The application must be submitted in the original, and any attachments must be submitted in the original or as a certified copy, no older than three months. The application for registration in the register is signed by the authorised representative of the office or representation of the foreign non-governmental organisation, or by the person authorised to carry out the registration. Along with the application for the registration of an office or representation of a foreign non-governmental organisation in the register with the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina, the following must be attached: a) a registration certificate or an extract from the register of a foreign non-governmental organisation in its country of origin, or another written document where registration of a non-governmental organisation is not required by the law of the country of origin; b) if the evidence from paragraph 1(a) of this article does not contain information on the statutory aims and activities of the foreign non-governmental organisation, it shall also attach the statute or any other internal act of the foreign non-governmental organisation, on the basis of which the Ministry determines the objectives and activities of the foreign non-governmental organisation, for the reason that the office or representation may carry out in Bosnia and Herzegovina only those registered objectives and activities, or part thereof; c) a decision by the competent body of the foreign non-governmental organisation on the opening of an office or a representative office in Bosnia and Herzegovina, which specifies: the objectives and activities, the seat and address, the name and surname of the person authorised to represent it, and a description of the seal and emblem of the office or representation in Bosnia and Herzegovina; d) a certified copy of the identity card of the person authorised to represent the office or representation of the foreign non-governmental organisation, and if that person is a foreign national, a certified copy of their passport and residence permit in Bosnia and Herzegovina; e) a certified signature of the person authorised to represent the office or representation of the foreign non-governmental organisation by the competent authority; f) proof of payment of the administrative fee. The annexes listed in paragraph 1(a), b) and c) of this Article must be certified by the competent authority with an apostille stamp in the country of origin and translated into one of the official languages in use in Bosnia and Herzegovina by an authorised court interpreter, and certified by the competent authority of Bosnia and Herzegovina, unless otherwise provided for by a special regulation.

title: How to register the office or representative office of a foreign non-governmental organization?
anchor_text: Offices or representative offices of foreign non-governmental organizations
text: How to register the office or representative office of a foreign non-governmental organization? A foreign or international association or foundation and another non-profit organisation (hereinafter: foreign non-governmental organisation) may register its office or representation in Bosnia and Herzegovina. The application for the registration of an office or representation of a foreign non-governmental organisation is submitted to the Ministry on Form 4. The application with attachments is completed on a computer, in Latin script or Cyrillic script, in one of the official languages of Bosnia and Herzegovina, within the relevant section. The request and its attachments must be submitted in a single copy. The application must be submitted in the original, and any attachments must be submitted in the original or as a certified photocopy no more than three months old. The application for registration in the Register is signed by the person authorised to represent the office or representation of the foreign non-governmental organisation, or by the entity authorised to carry out registration matters.

title: Entry in the Register, changes and deletion from the Register
anchor_text: Entry in the Register, changes and deletion from the Register
text: Entry in the Register, changes and deletion from the Register Application for enrolment Regulations on the manner of keeping the Register of legal entities established by institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina (hereinafter: the Regulation), prescribes the form and content of the Register, the form and content of the application for registration in the Register and the forms to be attached to the application, the registration procedure, the procedure for amendment and supplementation of the registration and other matters of significance for the registration and maintenance of the Register. Registration in the Register of Registrants, amendments to the data in the Register of Registrants, or the deletion of a registrant from the Register is carried out on the basis of a single application on Form 1, which the authorised representative of the registrant submits to the Ministry. The application contains the information that must be entered in the Register by law. The data are entered on the record sheets, on Form 2, and form an integral part of the application, depending on the type of data being recorded. The forms form an integral part of the Regulations and are available on the website of the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The forms include instructions on how to complete them. They must be filled in on a typewriter or computer, provided that they are not crossed out or erased. You can download the forms here. Form titles Form 1 Application for Registration in the Register of Legal Entities of Bosnia and Herzegovina Hereinafter: the Applicant Form 2 REGISTRATION SHEET Appendix 1 - Details of the name, registered office and form of the registered entity Appendix 2 - Details of the ownership (founders) of the registered entity Appendix 3 - The activity or business of the registered entity Annex 4 - Details of the person authorised to represent the registered entity in legal transactions Annex 5 - Details of the registered capital, contributions, shares and interests of the registered entity Appendix No. 6 - Details of the seal and logo of the registered entity Appendix No. 7 - List of members of the management board of the registered entity Appendix No. 8 - Information on the branch or other part of the registered entity Appendix No. 9 - Details of the person authorised to represent the branch in legal transactions Appendix 10 - Details of the cessation of operations of the registered entity Continuation (11) - Auxiliary record sheet Form 3 List of persons authorised to represent the entity registered in legal transactions Form 4 List of persons authorised to represent the entity registered in legal transactions Form 7 COPY FROM THE REGISTER Enrolment in Government For registration in the Register of Registrable Entities, the application must be accompanied by records sheets number 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5, and numbers 6 and 7 if the registrable entity uses a sign or seal, or has a board of directors, and if the registered entity has a branch or other parts with powers in legal transactions, numbers 8, 9 and 10 (if branches are established in the entity's registration documents) and the following documents and evidence are attached: the statute or founding act of the entity to be registered, if any, certified with the seal and/or signature of an authorised person; a document appointing a person to represent the registered entity and a certified signature of the person so appointed, on Form 3; instrument of appointment of the members of the Board of Directors, if any; a deed of appointment of the branch manager and the certified signature of the person authorised to represent; proof of payment of the fee; and other documents and evidence, if the Ministry determines that they are necessary for establishing the facts in the proceedings. If a branch is established after the registration of the entity in the Register, the application must be accompanied by amendments and additions to the articles of association establishing the branch, and by the documents referred to in point 4 of this Act. The application for registration, together with the attached records, must be submitted in two identical copies, signed by an authorised representative of the registrant, or by one of the authorised representatives, and certified with the registrant's seal (if used). Documents and evidence must be submitted in the original, as a certified transcript or as a certified copy, in a single copy. Legal entities registered in the Register in accordance with the provisions of the said Law carry out the activities established by their statute or founding act, in accordance with the Constitution and laws of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and have the right to operate throughout the entire territory of Bosnia and Herzegovina. Changes to register data Along with the application for the registration of a change of data arising at the registered entity, the following records 1 to 9 are attached, in which the data to be changed are specified: Registration of a change of name, registered office and activities, with the application is attached an amendment and supplement to the articles of association or the deed of incorporation, Registration of a change in the person authorised to represent the registered entity; the application must be accompanied by the document evidencing the change in the person authorised to represent the entity, on Form 4. Registration of changes to the board of directors; the application is accompanied by the deed of change to the board of directors. Proof of payment of the fee. Deletion from the register For registration of the cessation of operations of the registered entity and its deletion from the Register of Registered Entities, the application must be accompanied by Record Sheet No. 10 and the decision on the cessation of operations of the registered entity; for the application to delete a branch from the Register, the amendment and supplement to the articles of association must be attached as proof of the branch's cessation of operations.

title: Publicity of the Register
text: Publicity of the Register The register is public. Any interested party has the right to inspect the data contained in the Register of Registered Entities, with the exception of personal data of authorised representatives. The Ministry issues an extract from the Register based on the data. Any interested person may submit a request for any copy of a document from the Register to be provided to them within 15 days of the date the request is received by the Ministry.

title: Churches and religious communities
text: Churches and religious communities Registration in the Register of Churches and Religious Communities is carried out in accordance with the Law on Freedom of Religion and the Legal Status of Churches and Religious Communities in Bosnia and Herzegovina ("Official Gazette of BiH", no. 5/04) and the Regulation on the Establishment and Maintenance of the Unified Register for the Entry of Churches and Religious Communities, their Unions and Organisational Forms in Bosnia and Herzegovina («Official Gazette of BiH», no. 46/04). Churches and religious communities are communities, institutions and organisations of believers, established in accordance with their own regulations, teachings, beliefs, traditions and practices, which have been recognised as having legal capacity and are entered in the Register of Churches and Religious Communities in Bosnia and Herzegovina. The register of existing and new churches and religious communities, their unions and internal organisational forms (hereinafter: the Registered Entity), is maintained by the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. By the Law on Freedom of Religion and the Legal Status of Churches and Religious Communities in Bosnia and Herzegovina, the continuity of legal personality of historically established churches and religious communities in Bosnia and Herzegovina is confirmed in accordance with religious regulations and the internal organisation of the Islamic Community in BiH, the Serbian Orthodox Church, the Catholic Church and the Jewish Community of Bosnia and Herzegovina, as well as all other churches and religious communities, which had been recognised as legal entities prior to the entry into force of this law. New churches and religious communities acquire legal personality in the manner provided for by the Act and the Regulations.

title: Entering changes in the Registry
text: Entering changes in the Registry Churches and religious communities registered in the Register maintained by the Ministry of Justice are required to report any changes that have occurred with regard to: the name (and abbreviated name, if any) of the Enrollee; seats of the Enrolment Subject; of the content, description and seal of the Registered Entity; content, description, and stamp of the stamp; content and description of the log or sign of the registered entity, if any; persons authorised to represent The application to register any changes made is submitted on Form U-4.

title: Registration
text: Registration Both Churches and religious communities to which the continuity of legal personality is confirmed The application for registration in the Register of Registered Entities, confirming the continuity of the legal entity, is submitted to the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina on form U-1, signed by the person authorised to represent the Registered Entity. The U-2 form, which provides the details (first name, surname, handwritten signature, unique personal identification number and the service of the authorised representative) of the authorised representative, is attached to the application (Form U-1). This form is signed by an official. If a new organisational unit, which will have legal personality, is established within an existing church or religious community, the application for registration in the Register must be submitted on form U-1-1, signed by the person authorised to represent the new registrant. The following must be attached to the U-1-1 application: a) a duly completed U-1 form for an existing church or religious community, signed by a person authorised to represent the existing church or religious community; b) a decision by the competent authority of the church or religious community on the establishment of a new organisational unit (i.e. a new entity for registration); c) a U-2 form signed by an official, which contains details of the person authorised to represent the new registrant. Applications for registration in the Register and applications for registration of changes in the Register are submitted to the Ministry of Justice by: The Bishops' Conference and the diocesan ordinariates for the Catholic Church, the Eparchies for the Serbian Orthodox Church, the Riyasat of the Islamic Community in Bosnia and Herzegovina and the muftis' offices for the Islamic Community, and the Jewish Community of Bosnia and Herzegovina for the Jewish Community. II New churches or religious communities A new church or religious community may be established by 300 adult citizens of Bosnia and Herzegovina, regardless of their entity citizenship. A new church or religious community cannot be established under the same or a similar name as an existing church or religious community. The application for registration of a new church or religious community is submitted to the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina on form U-3, which is signed by the person authorised to represent the entity being registered. The following must be attached to the U-3 application (form): a) the statute of the church or religious community, from which the content and manner of professing the faith, the performance of religious rites, the scope and manner of its activities are apparent; b) a document concerning her official religious instruction; c) a list of 300 citizens – followers, containing the following information: first name and surname, place of birth, current residential address, unique personal identification number and the handwritten signature of the citizens – followers; d) the decision to establish, adopted by at least 30 founders of the church or religious community, the first name and surname of the head of the church or religious community, his deputy or other representatives authorised to represent the church or religious community before state authorities; e) a U-2 form signed by an official, which contains details of the person authorised to represent the registered entity. III Union of Churches and Religious Communities Two or more churches or religious communities from Bosnia and Herzegovina may establish alliances of churches and religious communities. Alliances are established by agreement, declaration or other written instrument in which the founders are named, the objectives for which the Alliance is established are defined, the manner of forming the joint governance and representative structure is set out, and the duration of the Alliance is specified. The application for the Association's registration in the Register is submitted on Form U-1, which is signed by the person authorised to represent the Association. Form U-2, containing the details of the person authorised to represent the Association and signed by an official, is also attached. The written act establishing the Association is likewise attached.

title: Deletion from the Register at the request of associations and foundations
anchor_text: Deletion from the Register at the request of associations and foundations
text: Deletion from the Register at the request of associations and foundations A registered association or foundation must submit a request for its deletion from the register of associations or foundations on Form 3 if the competent body of the association or foundation has adopted a decision on its dissolution or a decision on its merger or division. Along with the application for registration of the deletion from the register of associations or foundations, the applicant shall attach: a) the decision of the competent body of the association or foundation to cease its activities, in accordance with Articles 50 and 51a of the Act and the provisions of the association's or foundation's statutes; b) a plan for the liquidation of the association or foundation, together with a decision on the distribution of the association's or foundation's assets and funds, in accordance with its statutes and the Act; c) bank statement showing the balance of cash funds in the giro account of the entity making the subscription; d) proof from the competent tax authority of the settlement of liabilities; e) the minutes of the meeting of the association's assembly or the foundation's Board of Directors, and f) proof of payment of the administrative fee. The person authorised to represent the association or foundation must, within 30 days of the date on which the association or foundation ceased to operate, submit to the Ministry an application with evidence of its cessation of activities. The request for deletion from the register is completed electronically, in Latin script or Cyrillic script, in one of the three official languages in use in Bosnia and Herzegovina, in the appropriate fields. The application and its attachments must be submitted in the original or as a certified copy no older than three months, in a single copy. A foreign non-governmental organisation must submit an application for deletion from the register, office or representation of the foreign non-governmental organisation in Bosnia and Herzegovina on Form 4, when the competent authority of the foreign non-governmental organisation in its country of domicile has made a decision to cease the operation of the office or representation in Bosnia and Herzegovina. The request for deletion from the register is accompanied by: a) a decision by the competent authority of the foreign non-governmental organisation to cease the activities of its office or representation in Bosnia and Herzegovina, certified by the competent authority in its country of domicile bearing an apostille and translated into one of the official languages in use in Bosnia and Herzegovina, unless otherwise provided for by a special regulation, by an authorised court interpreter, and certified by the competent authority of Bosnia and Herzegovina, not older than three months; b) proof from the competent tax authority of the settlement of liabilities, and c) proof of payment of the administrative fee. The request for the deletion from the register of an office or representation of a foreign non-governmental organisation is signed by the person authorised to represent the office or representation of the foreign non-governmental organisation in Bosnia and Herzegovina, and is certified with a seal. The application must be submitted in the original, and any attachments must be submitted in the original or as a certified copy no older than three months.

title: Exam requirements
text: Exam requirements In accordance with Article 2 of the Act on the Taking of the Judicial Examination (hereinafter: the Act), the examination may be taken by persons who, as qualified lawyers, have worked for at least two years in legal professional roles. Under the same conditions, the examination may also be taken by trainees and volunteers.

title: Application for passing the bar exam
text: Application for passing the bar exam The application for sitting the judicial examination is submitted to the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina, Trg BiH No. 1, Sarajevo, either in person or by post. As evidence that the candidate meets the conditions set out in Article 2 of the Law, the candidate shall attach to the application the original or certified copies of: Degrees from a faculty of law (including degrees from all cycles of study); certificates or certificates of professional experience in legal matters (at least two years), after obtaining the degree of a graduate lawyer (prior to the higher education reform), or after completing a four-year study programme at a law faculty in BiH in accordance with the Bologna Process; Certificates or statements of passed examinations from higher education (including transcripts for all cycles of study); Decisions on the recognition and evaluation of a foreign educational qualification (nostrification of a diploma) Extract from the register of marriages in the event of a change of surname. A certificate of completed practical training for persons referred to in Article 3 of the Act (volunteers) is issued by the president of the court, or by the bar association with which the person is entered in the register as a trainee lawyer. In the application, the candidate may specify the date on which they wish to sit the bar exam. The candidate must indicate a telephone number on which they can be contacted.

Municipality code of the payer: must include the code of the municipality of the payer's residence or registered office. Invitation to call: fill in with the numbers "0" (zero) The candidate is required to make the payment of the specified amount immediately upon receipt of the decision confirming they meet the conditions for sitting the judicial examination and the notification of the examination date, and to send the proof of payment by fax to: 033/ 201-625, (by the date specified in the notification), otherwise the Commission will not be convened for the specified date. The procedure for cancelling an appointment is clarified in the Regulations on the Taking of the Judicial Examination at the Level of Bosnia and Herzegovina.

title: Method of passing the bar exam
anchor_text: How to take the bar exam
text: Method of passing the bar exam The exam is taken in writing and orally. The written part of the exam covers criminal law and civil law. The oral part of the exam is taken in: Criminal law (substantive and procedural) Civil law (substantive and procedural) Family law Economic law Administrative law Labour law Constitutional system and organisation of the judiciary. The examination is taken as part of the bar examination programme, which includes the syllabus, legal sources and literature for each subject from 1) to 7). The programme for the judicial examination at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina was adopted and published in the Official Gazette of Bosnia and Herzegovina, No. 11/19. The examination is taken before the Bar Examination Board, which consists of a chairperson and four members drawn from the panel of examiners. The Ministry of Justice determines by decision whether the candidate meets the requirements to sit the bar exam. On the basis of the decision authorising the candidate to sit the exam, the Chair of the Commission fixes the time at which the candidate will sit the exam. The process of sitting the bar exam On the first day of the examination period, a written essay (judgment) in criminal law is taken; on the second day, one in civil law. The candidate is entitled to six hours to complete the written assignment. On the third day, the oral examination takes place. A candidate who fails the written part of the examination in either the criminal law or civil law section cannot proceed to the oral examination and is deemed to have failed the examination. The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina issues a certificate of passing to the candidate who has passed the examination, which is signed by the Minister of Justice. Make-up exam A candidate who does not demonstrate satisfactory knowledge in up to two subjects in the examination is entitled to sit a resit examination in those subjects, or in that subject. The period for sitting a resit examination in one subject shall be no less than one month and no more than three months, and the period for sitting a resit examination in two subjects shall be no less than two months and no more than four months. If a candidate fails only the oral component of an examination in a subject in which they have already passed the written component, they are not required to sit the written component again at the resit. If the candidate does not pass the resit exam within the specified period, they will be deemed to have failed the exam. A candidate who has failed the exam may retake it after a period of six months from the date of the previous sitting. Withdrawal from an exam If a candidate fails to sit the exam or, before it begins, declares that they are withdrawing from it, they will be deemed not to have sat the exam. In this case, the candidate must submit a new application to sit the exam. If a candidate withdraws from an exam that has already begun, or fails to submit their written work within the allotted time, they will be deemed to have failed the exam. Exam postponement The start of an examination may be postponed if a candidate is prevented from continuing due to illness or other justified reasons. The decision to postpone the examination is made by the Commission. The decision on resuming the examination is made by the Commission at the candidate's request. The request must be submitted within eight days of the reason for the postponement ceasing, but no later than three months from the date the decision to postpone was made. If the candidate does not submit the request to resume the examination within the deadline, or if their request is refused, they shall be deemed to have failed the examination. A candidate may appeal against the Commission's decision to the Minister of Justice within 15 days of receiving the decision. Upon resuming the examination, the candidate will not sit the part of the examination they were taking at the time of the postponement. Public examination Article 14 of the Act regulates that the oral part of the judicial examination is public. Other interested candidates, as well as other individuals, may attend a candidate's oral examination, subject to space capacity. The examination is taken at the premises of the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. title: Method of taking the professional administrative exam for higher education
text: Method of taking the professional administrative exam for higher education The examination is taken in writing and orally. The written part covers the administrative procedure and administrative dispute. The written part lasts one hour. After passing the written part, the candidate proceeds to the oral examination on the subjects set out in the Programme. The examination is held at the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina before the Commission for the professional administrative examination for higher education, appointed by the Minister of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The oral part of the professional administrative examination is public. For candidates who meet the requirements to sit the examination, the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina issues a decision approving their admission to the professional examination. The same decision sets the examination date. The decision is sent to candidates by post. A candidate who does not demonstrate satisfactory knowledge in no more than two subjects in the oral part of the professional examination is entitled to sit a remedial examination in those subjects. The remedial examination must be sat no earlier than 30 days and no later than 90 days from the date of the professional examination. When sitting the professional examination or a resit, candidates must present an identity document and proof of payment of the examination fee. title: Requirements for taking the professional administrative exam for a university degree
text: Requirements for taking the professional administrative exam for a university degree In accordance with the Decision, candidates with higher education are required to sit the professional administrative examination. Trainees with higher education who work in the administrative bodies of Bosnia and Herzegovina, in the services, bodies and institutions of the Council of Ministers of Bosnia and Herzegovina and in institutions with public authority, upon completion of a one-year traineeship. Employees with higher education in the administrative bodies of Bosnia and Herzegovina, the services, bodies and institutions of the Council of Ministers, and institutions with public powers. In order to meet the conditions for work in administrative and other professional positions in the administrative bodies of BiH, the services, bodies and institutions of the Council of Ministers and institutions with public authority, professional examination may also be taken by persons employed in other bodies of authority and institutions, or in companies and other legal entities on the territory of Bosnia and Herzegovina, provided that, after completing a higher education, they have worked in those bodies and other legal entities for at least one year.

title: Submitting a request to take the professional administrative exam for higher education
text: Submitting a request to take the professional administrative exam for higher education The application to sit the professional management examination must be submitted in writing to the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina, at 1 Bosne i Hercegovine Square. The following must be attached to the application: Proof of completed higher education (diploma or certificate of graduation, original or certified copies). A diploma obtained outside Bosnia and Herzegovina after 6 April 1992 must be accompanied by proof of its nostrification. Proof of the required professional work experience after completing higher education (at least one year of work experience). Certified copy of the birth register for individuals who changed their surname after graduating. Note: No specific form or prescribed application form is required for submitting a request to sit a resit examination and a request to resit the professional administrative examination, except that the request must refer to or state the number and date of the decision approving the candidate to sit the professional administrative examination, the date of the examination, and the fact that the candidate failed one or two papers, for which they were referred to the remedial examination. The application is not accompanied by previously submitted evidence of meeting the requirements for sitting the professional management examination.

title: Basic information
text: Basic information On this page you can find all the information regarding the professional management examination for secondary and upper secondary school qualifications, from the eligibility criteria, relevant regulations, syllabus and literature, to the associated costs and current examination dates. RIGHT OF PLACEMENT Secondary school qualification. A person who has six months' professional work experience – meaning work experience gained after completing secondary school in roles requiring the qualification they hold. Higher and tertiary education A person who has one year's professional work experience – meaning work experience gained after completing higher or tertiary education in a role requiring the educational qualification they hold. Note: It is not necessary for the work experience to have been gained in the administrative bodies of BiH, the services, bodies and institutions of the Council of Ministers. Conditions for sitting the examination The candidate is required to submit the following documents to the Ministry of Justice: Written request for sitting - the form can be found on the website of the Ministry of Justice of BiH Proof of completed schooling – a certified copy of the diploma or certificate of completion of secondary education. If the diploma was obtained outside Bosnia and Herzegovina after 6 April 1992, a certificate of its nostrification must also be provided. Proof of completion of a traineeship, or of professional work experience – to be provided by a certificate or confirmation issued by the employer with whom the candidate gained the required work experience. - example of a certificate/confirmation For female applicants who have changed their surname since graduating or finishing secondary school, it is necessary to also provide a birth certificate. proof of payment of 300.00 KM (candidates must submit the proof of payment no later than three days from the date of receipt of the Decision in order to attend the appointment) The application for the examination, together with the required documentation, may be submitted by post to the address: Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina, Trg BiH 3, 71000 Sarajevo, or delivered to the protocol office of the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. Note: The certificate or confirmation of work experience must state that the candidate has gained the required work experience in the educational qualification for which they are applying to sit the professional examination. If the candidate submits a proper application, the Minister of Justice of BiH will issue a decision approving the sitting of the professional examination, which will specify the date, time and place of the examination. The decision is sent to the candidate by registered post, and the list of candidates and the examination date are published on the Ministry of Justice's website. NOTE: The candidate is required to sit the professional examination at the date and time specified in the Ministry's notice. A candidate who has postponed the professional examination must pay an additional 25% of the established fee for each subsequent examination date approved. A candidate may postpone a scheduled professional examination date by written request, up to a maximum of twice within 60 days of the original date. If the candidate does not sit the professional examination at the scheduled time even after the second postponement, they shall be deemed to have withdrawn their application to sit the examination and shall forfeit any right to a refund of the examination fee paid. A candidate who fails one or two subjects in the professional management examination is entitled to sit a resit for those subjects, no earlier than 30 days and no later than 90 days from the date of the professional examination. A candidate who has not passed the professional administrative examination may retake it after a period of 60 days from the date of the examination. LAYING PROGRAM The professional examination covers the following subjects: a) Foundations of the constitutional system and European integration, b) Principles of administration in BiH, c) Administrative procedure and administrative litigation d) Employment relations, e) Office administration in the administrative bodies of BiH, and in the services, bodies and institutions of the Council of Ministers of BiH. Note: The programme for the professional examination can be found on the website of the Ministry of Justice of BiH. Structure of the professional examination The professional examination consists of a written and an oral part. The written part of the examination represents the practical application of legal provisions. When selecting tasks for the written section of the professional examination, candidates with higher education are given a written assignment on the most complex duties, those with upper secondary education on complex duties, and those with secondary school education on simpler administrative matters. When preparing the written assignment, the candidate may only use laws and subordinate regulations. The oral part of the examination is taken before an Examination Board consisting of five members, appointed by the Minister of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. Note: If a candidate fails to sit the professional examination on the day designated for it, or if they declare their withdrawal from the examination on the day before the scheduled date, they shall be deemed not to have sat the examination. Certificate of Passed Examination The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina issues a certificate of passing the professional examination to the candidate who has passed it, which is sent by registered post.

title: Literature
text: Literature Guides for Sitting the Bar Examination To date, four preparatory manuals for the Judicial Examination have been published, prepared in accordance with the Programme for the Judicial Examination at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina and the Programme for the Judicial Examination at the level of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina. They are: 1. COMMERCIAL LAW 2. ADMINISTRATIVE AND LABOR LAW 3. CONSTITUTIONAL SYSTEM AND ORGANIZATION OF THE JUDICIARY IN BIH 4. CIVIL LAW The author of three handbooks (Commercial Law, Administrative Law and the Constitutional System and Organisation of the Judiciary in BiH) is Mato Tadić, an examiner on the Commissions at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina and at the level of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The author of the handbook for the judicial examination in Civil Law is Bariša Čolak, an examiner on the Commission for the judicial examination at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The publisher of the manual is the Public Enterprise NIO "Official Gazette of BiH". title: Conditions for taking the professional administrative exam for secondary and higher education
text: Conditions for taking the professional administrative exam for secondary and higher education In accordance with the Decision, candidates with a secondary and higher school education are required to sit the professional administrative examination. Trainees in the administrative bodies of Bosnia and Herzegovina, in the services, bodies and institutions of the Council of Ministers and in institutions with public powers, upon completion of a traineeship of one year for those with higher education, or six months for those with secondary education. In order to meet the conditions for work in administrative and other professional positions in the administrative bodies of BiH, In order to meet the requirements for employment in administrative and other professional posts in the administrative bodies, services, bodies and institutions of the Council of Ministers and in institutions with public powers, professional examinations may also be taken by persons employed in other bodies of authority and institutions, or in enterprises (companies) and other legal entities on the territory of Bosnia and Herzegovina. These individuals may sit the professional examination once they have, following completion of higher secondary education, spent at least one year in employment with those bodies, institutions, enterprises (companies) and other legal entities; and at least six months following completion of secondary school education.

title: Basic information
text: Basic information The professional administrative examination for secondary and higher education is taken in accordance with the Decision on the conditions and manner of taking the professional administrative examination for trainees, persons with higher education and employees at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina ("Official Gazette of BiH" No. 104/10), the Decision on Amendments and Supplements to the Decision on the Conditions and Method of Taking the Professional Administrative Examination for Trainees, Persons with Higher Education and Employees at the Level of Bosnia and Herzegovina ("Official Gazette of BiH" No. 105/12) and Decision on the Supplement to the Decision on the Conditions and Procedure for Sitting the Professional Administrative Examination for Trainees, Persons with Higher Education and Employees at the Level of Bosnia and Herzegovina ("Official Gazette of BiH" no. 61/14). The examination is taken in accordance with the Programme for the Professional Administrative Examination for Trainees, Higher-Education Graduates and Employees at the Level of Bosnia and Herzegovina (Official Gazette of BiH No. 12/13), which prescribes the content, syllabus and legal sources for sitting the professional administrative examination. In accordance with the Law on Work in the Institutions of BiH ("Official Gazette" no. 26/04) and the aforementioned Decision, the examination is mandatory for trainees and employees with secondary and higher education employed in the administrative bodies of BiH, services, bodies and institutions of the Council of Ministers of BiH and in enterprises (companies) and other legal entities entrusted by the law of Bosnia and Herzegovina with the performance of public authority. The professional administrative examination is taken in order to meet the requirements for work in administrative and other professional positions within the core activities of administrative bodies and other bodies at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina, or in administrative positions in institutions that have public powers, in accordance with the regulation on core activities, or on auxiliary and technical duties of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The professional administrative examination for candidates with secondary and higher education is taken upon fulfilling the requirements for this examination and upon the application of the interested candidate. The application is submitted to the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina.

title: Strategic planning, aid coordination and European integration
anchor_text: Strategic planning, aid coordination and European integration
text: Strategic planning, aid coordination and European integration The strategic objectives of the Ministry of Justice of BiH, particularly the reforms conditioned by the European integration process, are achieved through the creation and coordination of comprehensive plans and policies in the justice sector in Bosnia and Herzegovina. The reform of the justice sector also entails the precise planning and coordination of assistance from the EU and other donor countries. Strategic planning Coordination of aid European integration Strategic planning Through strategic planning and coordination of assistance, the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina introduces modern techniques of organisational management and defines the priorities the organisation sets with the aim of achieving measurable objectives. The strategic planning process aims to provide a systematic and sustainable approach to prioritisation, planning and resource allocation, ensuring links between strategic and operational plans, budgets and monitoring and evaluation systems. The Department for Strategic Planning and Coordination of Assistance provides expert administrative support for the development of sectoral strategies (the Strategy for the Reform of the Justice Sector in BiH), the preparation of institutional strategic plans, the preparation of annual operational plans, and supports the Ministry's leadership in establishing mechanisms for monitoring and performance evaluation. Coordination of aid Within the relevant sector, project proposals for technical and other assistance to strengthen judicial capacities and implement necessary reforms are regularly monitored and prepared. The Ministry has established a system for collecting, linking and prioritising the needs of institutions in the justice sector. Alongside regular donor coordination, the Ministry maintains a database of donor assistance to the justice sector. European integration The Department for European Integration coordinates the implementation of strategies and policies on European integration within the remit of the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. In the European integration process, the ministry's objectives are realised by aligning the legal system and regulations with the acquis, as well as by implementing the acquis within the Ministry of Justice's remit.

text: Exemption from taking the professional administrative exam/recognition of the professional administrative exam On this page you can find information regarding exemptions from the professional administrative examination, or the recognition of the professional administrative examination for secondary, upper secondary and tertiary education qualifications. Recognition of previously passed professional examinations, or exemption from the obligation to sit a professional examination at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina, is carried out on the basis of a comparison of the syllabus under which the person sat the professional examination and Decision on the conditions and procedure for sitting the professional management examination for trainees, holders of higher education qualifications and employees at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina Decision on Amendments and Supplementations to the Decision on the Conditions and Procedure for Sitting the Professional Administrative Examination for Trainees, Persons with Higher Education and Employees at the Level of Bosnia and Herzegovina Decision on the amendment of the Decision on the conditions and procedure for sitting the professional administrative examination for trainees, holders of higher education qualifications and employees at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina Programme for the professional administrative examination for trainees, higher-education graduates and employees at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina Upon written request for exemption from sitting the professional management examination / recognition of the professional management examination, the interested party shall submit the following documentation: A certified photocopy of the Certificate of Passed Professional Examination; plan and programme of the examination passed within other professions; extract from the register of births, in the case of a change of surname, and Proof of payment of the administrative fee.

title: Jurisdictions
text: Jurisdictions The Ministry of Justice was established in 2003, by the Law on Ministries and Other Administrative Bodies of Bosnia and Herzegovina ("Official Gazette of BiH", no. 5/2003), and is responsible for administrative functions related to judicial bodies at the state level; international and inter-entity judicial cooperation (mutual legal assistance and contacts with international tribunals); the enactment of appropriate laws and regulations to regulate the matters referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 of this article; ensuring that the legislation of BiH and its implementation at all levels are in accordance with the obligations of BiH arising from international agreements; cooperation with the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and entities in the drafting of international bilateral and multilateral agreements; providing guidance and monitoring legal education, in order to ensure inter-entity harmonisation in this area and compliance with the best standards; generally acting as a central coordinating body for ensuring the conformity of legislation and standards of the judicial system among the entities, either by providing the conditions for discussion or by coordinating initiatives; extradition; administrative inspection duties over the enforcement of laws relating to civil servants and employees of administrative bodies, administrative procedure, and special administrative procedures and office management in administrative bodies; Matters concerning citizens' associations, and the keeping of registers of citizens' associations and non-governmental organisations operating in the territory of BiH; other tasks and duties which are not within the remit of other ministries of BiH, and are related to the tasks within the remit of this ministry.

title: Administrative inspection
text: Administrative inspection The regulations the implementation of which is overseen by the Administrative Inspectorate are: The Law on Administration and the subordinate acts adopted on the basis of the powers conferred by that law; Law on Ministries and Other Administrative Bodies of Bosnia and Herzegovina The Law on Public Service in the Institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina and the subordinate acts adopted on the basis of the powers conferred by that law; The Law on Work in Institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina and the subordinate acts adopted on the basis of the powers conferred by that law; Administrative Procedure Act Decision on the office management of the ministries, services, institutions and other bodies of the Council of Ministers of Bosnia and Herzegovina; Instructions on the conduct of office business in the institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina; as well as other regulations whose provisions stipulate that the Administrative Inspectorate is competent to supervise their implementation. 1. Initiation of administrative inspection supervision The administrative inspector, by virtue of their office, initiates and conducts the inspection oversight. Any natural or legal person has the right to submit a request for an administrative inspection to oversee the application of laws and other regulations whose enforcement is within the remit of the Administrative Inspectorate. 2. Carrying out administrative inspection oversight Upon carrying out an inspection, the acting administrative inspector draws up an inspection report which must include the established factual situation, as well as any irregularities and deficiencies in the work. A copy of the report is sent to the head of the administrative body or other authority and, where necessary, to the Council of Ministers. The administrative inspector shall notify the applicant in writing of the measures taken within the legally prescribed period. If the administrative inspector finds that the irregularities and shortcomings in work are the result of the incompetence of the authorised official in carrying out the duties to which they have been assigned, they shall propose to the head of the administrative authority or other body that the official be reassigned to other duties. 3. Making a decision If an administrative inspector, in carrying out an inspection, finds that a law or other regulation has been breached, they are obliged to issue a decision and, by that decision, order: that the identified deficiencies and irregularities be rectified within a specified period; that the appropriate administrative actions which the administrative authority or other body is required to take be undertaken; or that other administrative measures and actions for which it is authorised by a special law or other regulation be taken. 4. Appealing the decision Against the decision of the administrative inspector, the head of an administrative body or another authority may, within eight days of receipt of the decision, lodge an appeal with the Minister of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. An appeal against the administrative inspector's decision suspends its enforcement. The Minister of Justice's decision is final and may be challenged in administrative proceedings before the Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina. 5. Obligations of the management body and other bodies towards the Administrative Inspection Every administrative authority and other bodies whose work is subject to administrative inspection oversight are obliged to allow the administrative inspector to carry out the inspection and to provide them with any necessary notices and information relevant to its conclusion. 6. The relationship of the Administrative Inspectorate to the Council of Ministers If the administrative inspector determines that violations of the law and other regulations are preventing natural and legal persons from exercising their rights and legitimate interests within the prescribed deadlines, or in any other way, the administrative inspector is obliged to inform the Council of Ministers of this in writing without delay, to point out the consequences that have arisen or could arise from such a situation, and to propose the taking of appropriate measures.

title: Public consultation
text: Public consultation The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina is the first state body in Bosnia and Herzegovina to have begun implementing the Rules for Consultations in the Preparation of Legal Regulations of the Council of Ministers of BiH. All interested citizens and legal entities have the opportunity to participate directly in the drafting of legal regulations. Through the public consultation process, we aim to ensure a greater level of participation by interested parties in legislative activities, better informed citizens, and greater public trust in the strategic policy-making process. title: Strategic planning
text: Strategic planning Through strategic planning and coordination of assistance, the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina introduces modern techniques of organisational management and defines the priorities the organisation sets with the aim of achieving measurable objectives. The strategic planning process aims to provide a systematic and sustainable approach to setting priorities, planning and allocating resources, ensuring links between strategic and operational plans, budgets and monitoring and evaluation systems. The Department for Strategic Planning and Assistance Coordination provides expert-administrative support for the development of sectoral strategies (Strategy for the Reform of the Justice Sector in BiH), the development of& institutional strategic plans, the preparation of annual operational plans, and provides support to the Ministry's management in establishing mechanisms for monitoring and performance evaluation. Within the relevant sector, project proposals for technical and other assistance to strengthen judicial capacities and implement necessary reforms are regularly monitored and prepared. The Ministry has established a system for collecting, linking and prioritising the needs of institutions in the justice sector. Alongside regular donor coordination, the Ministry maintains a database of donor assistance to the justice sector. Strategy for the reform of the justice sector Institutional strategy

title: European integration
text: European integration The Department for European Integration coordinates the implementation of strategies and policies on European integration within the remit of the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. In the European integration process, the ministry's objectives are realised by aligning the legal system and regulations with the acquis, as well as by implementing the acquis within the Ministry of Justice's remit. title: Civil society
text: Civil society Within its legal powers, the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina cooperates with the non-governmental sector and ensures the development of civil society. This area of the Ministry's work was established because civil society and the development of NGOs are recognised as an important element in the public and future European life of Bosnia and Herzegovina. Cooperation and development of civil society Europe for Citizens Programme Public consultations - e-Consultations Agreement on Cooperation between the Council of Ministers of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Non-Governmental Organisations in Bosnia and Herzegovina Advisory Body of the Council of Ministers of BiH for Cooperation with Non-Governmental Organisations Cooperation and development of civil society By establishing the Sector for Legal Aid and Civil Society Development, the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina has ensured the prerequisites for the adoption of legislation and strategies favourable to the development of civil society. The development of civil society, namely the non-governmental, non-profit, voluntary and other sector, which is characterised by freedom of association and action for the common good, is also one of the priorities of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the process of European integration. The Sector's priorities include, among other things: creating a more favourable environment for the development of civil society, preparing and proposing various forms of cooperation between Bosnia and Herzegovina and the Council of Ministers of BiH, and improving the work of existing bodies, encouraging the participation of civil society in the public consultation process when drafting legal regulations, preparing accompanying periodic analyses and reports on cooperation and compliance with legislation relating to the civil sector, etc. The Ministry will provide support in training civil society organisations on accessing EU funds, facilitating European networking, strengthening the capacity of small organisations, etc. Europe for Citizens Programme (https://eacea.ec.europa.eu/europe-for-citizens) The European Union is made up of its citizens and was created for the benefit of its citizens. It is of great importance to support and enable greater citizen participation in the European Union and in what the European Union represents. This ranges from the need for greater citizen involvement in existing activities to the need to foster a better understanding of the history of the European Union and its origins after the two World Wars. In this sense, the Europe for Citizens programme (the Programme) is an important instrument intended to ensure that the Union's 500 million inhabitants play a greater role in its development through financial programmes and activities in which citizens can participate. The Programme promotes a common European history and values and encourages a sense of responsibility for the future development of the Union. The Programme should be accessible to all citizens of the European Union without any discrimination on the grounds of sex, racial or ethnic origin, religion or belief, disability, age and sexual orientation. The Programme's participating countries are the 28 members of the European Union, Albania, North Macedonia, Serbia, Montenegro and Bosnia and Herzegovina. Bosnia and Herzegovina's contact point for the Programme is the Sector for Legal Aid and Civil Society Development of the Ministry of Justice of BiH. Main activities Contact points: Organisation of information days, workshops and targeted meetings for potential users of the Programme. Providing information about the Programme by email and telephone Providing administrative and technical support to potential users of the Programme during the project application process. Updating the website with all relevant information about the Programme (including maintaining the tool for searching for partner organisations to implement projects) Production, printing and distribution of promotional materials. Public consultations - e-Consultations The Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina is the first state body in Bosnia and Herzegovina to have begun implementing the Rules for Consultations in the Preparation of Legal Regulations of the Council of Ministers of BiH. All interested citizens and legal entities have the opportunity to participate directly in the drafting of legal regulations. Through the public consultation process, we aim to ensure a higher level of participation from interested parties in legislative activities, better informed citizens, and greater public trust in the strategic policy-making process. By clicking the link below, you can access the web platform and register to participate in the e-consultations: https://www.ekonsultacije.gov.ba/ Agreement on Cooperation between the Council of Ministers of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Non-Governmental Organisations in Bosnia and Herzegovina The Agreement on Cooperation between the Council of Ministers of Bosnia and Herzegovina and non-governmental organisations in Bosnia and Herzegovina (the Agreement) was signed on 30 November 2017 in the building of the Parliamentary Assembly of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The signatories of the Agreement have committed to dedicating all their capacities to building a prosperous, just, open, pluralistic and democratic society in Bosnia and Herzegovina. Furthermore, the signatories confirmed their commitment to cooperate in fulfilling the conditions for Bosnia and Herzegovina's full membership of the European Union. This Agreement seeks to define priorities that will better correspond with the new challenges for BiH and the opportunities for cooperation between the Council of Ministers of BiH and non-governmental organisations. This creates the conditions for the creation of quality public policies and their effective implementation. Subsequent accession to the Agreement: Article 2 of the Agreement provides that non-governmental organisations may accede to the Agreement subsequently by notifying in writing the Sector for Legal Aid and Civil Society Development of the Ministry of Justice of Bosnia and Herzegovina. In this regard, to accede to the Agreement subsequently, it is necessary to complete Form 1 – Subsequent Accession to the Agreement and send it by e-mail or post. More about this at: http://www.mpr.gov.ba/nvo/archive.aspx?langTag=bs-BA&template_id=116&pageIndex=1 Advisory Body of the Council of Ministers of BiH for Cooperation with Non-Governmental Organisations On 1 December 2020, the constitutive session of the Advisory Body of the Council of Ministers of BiH for Cooperation with Non-Governmental Organisations was held. The establishment of the Advisory Body of the Council of Ministers of BiH for Cooperation with Non-Governmental Organisations fulfils one of the most significant commitments from the Cooperation Agreement between the Council of Ministers of BiH and non-governmental organisations in BiH. Furthermore, this step also contributes to fulfilling one of the 14 key priorities from the European Commission's Opinion on Bosnia and Herzegovina's application for membership of the European Union, which concerns ensuring a conducive environment for civil society in Bosnia and Herzegovina. In this way, the Council of Ministers of BiH has once again demonstrated its commitment to building a prosperous, fair, open, pluralistic and democratic society in BiH. In accordance with the Cooperation Agreement between the Council of Ministers of BiH and non-governmental organisations in BiH, the Advisory Body of the Council of Ministers of BiH for Cooperation with Non-Governmental Organisations is responsible for enhancing cooperation between the Council of Ministers of BiH and non-governmental organisations, with the aim of creating a conducive legal, institutional and financial environment for the operation of the non-governmental sector in BiH. The Advisory Body of the Council of Ministers of BiH for Cooperation with Non-Governmental Organisations is composed of seven members with long-standing experience in creating, monitoring and implementing public policies of importance for creating a stimulating legal, institutional and financial environment for non-governmental organisations in BiH, selected through a public call by the Ministry of Justice of BiH.

title: Free legal aid
text: Free legal aid Free legal aid is provided to users of free legal aid in procedures in which their rights and legally based interests are exercised and protected. Procedures in which free legal aid is provided administrative procedure; administrative dispute; civil proceedings; executive procedure; criminal procedure in accordance with the laws on criminal procedure in the context of realization of property claims before the court of Bosnia and Herzegovina Then, in accordance with the provisions of the Law on the provision of free legal aid, beneficiaries of free legal aid also exercise their rights in proceedings under extraordinary legal remedies, proceedings before the Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina and the European Court of Human Rights, as well as in proceedings before other international bodies for the protection of human rights, unless otherwise stipulated by an international treaty. Forms of free legal aid All persons have the right to general information about rights and obligations and assistance in filling out the application forms for exercising the right to free legal aid, regardless of whether they meet the conditions prescribed in Article 15 of the Law on the Provision of Free Legal Aid. Forms of help: legal advice; legal assistance in drafting all types of submissions; representation before administrative bodies and administrative organizations and institutions of Bosnia and Herzegovina; representation before the Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina; drawing up appeals; legal assistance in peaceful dispute resolution procedures (mediation); compilation of submissions submitted to international bodies for the protection of human rights. Beneficiaries of free legal aid a citizen of Bosnia and Herzegovina and another natural person residing on the territory of Bosnia and Herzegovina; a natural person who is on the territory of Bosnia and Herzegovina under international protection in accordance with international standards, especially asylum seekers, refugees, persons under subsidiary or temporary protection, persons in the process of expulsion, stateless persons, victims of human trafficking, in accordance with the obligations that Bosnia and Herzegovina has under international conventions; a person whose rights are protected by the provisions of the Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction, the Convention on the Recovery of Maintenance Claims Abroad, the Convention on Civil and Judicial Procedure and the Convention on Facilitating Access to Courts. Conditions for obtaining free legal aid The right to free legal aid based on status is exercised by: a person receiving social assistance; a child in accordance with the Convention on the Rights of the Child; a person deprived of legal capacity and a mentally ill person; a pension recipient who is lower than the average salary earned at the level of Bosnia and Herzegovina; a person who is unemployed and has no other regular income and income; a victim of domestic violence or gender-based violence; an asylum seeker, a person under subsidiary or temporary protection, a person in the process of expulsion, a stateless person and a victim of human trafficking. A person with poor financial status is also entitled to free legal aid when this is determined by the competent body for providing free legal aid.

title: Јавне консултације
text: Јавне консултације Министарство правде Босне и Херцеговине је прво државно тијело у Босни и Херцеговини које је почело провођење Правила за консултације у изради правних прописа Вијећа министара БиХ. Сви заинтересирани грађани и правне особе имају прилику директно судјеловати у изради правних прописа. Процесом јавних конзултација желимо осигурати већу разину судјеловања интересних група у законодавним активностима, бољу информираност и веће повјерење грађана у процес израде стратешких политика. Кликом на линк испод можете приступити wеб платформи и регистрирати се за учешће у е-консултацијама: хттпс://www.еконсултације.гов.ба/
